mission.title: Install and Configure Litmus on Kubernetes
mission.description: Litmus is an open-source Chaos Engineering platform that enables teams to identify weaknesses and potential outages in infrastructures by inducing chaos tests in a controlled manner. Installing Litmus allows SREs and developers to practice chaos engineering effectively in a cloud-native environment.

steps.title: Add the Litmus Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Litmus Helm repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add litmuschaos https://litmuschaos.github.io/litmus-helm-charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Litmus using Helm
steps.description:
Install Litmus in a new namespace called 'litmus' with the following command:
```bash
helm install litmus litmuschaos/litmus --namespace litmus --create-namespace --version 3.26.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Litmus pods are running correctly by executing:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace litmus
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
To ensure that Litmus has adequate resources, edit the deployment to set resource limits. Use the following command to edit the chaos operator deployment:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment litmus-operator --namespace litmus
``` 
In the editor, add resource limits under the containers section:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"
```

steps.title: Access the Litmus dashboard
steps.description:
To access the Litmus dashboard, set up port forwarding with the following command:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/litmus-frontend --namespace litmus 8080:80
``` 
You can now access the dashboard at http://localhost:8080.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Litmus will show all Litmus pods in the 'litmus' namespace as running. You will also be able to access the Litmus dashboard through port forwarding.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add litmuschaos https://litmuschaos.github.io/litmus-helm-charts
helm repo update

resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
